---
title: 'Written Resources: Scholars & Thinkers'
date: '2022-08-18T17:26:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_type: 'PAGE'
blogger_blog: 'NoHiding.Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_page_id: '8053197098868489588'
blogger_filename: '/p/resources-scholars-thinkers.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/NoHiding.Faith/feed.atom'
---

<p>&nbsp;</p><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;"><h3 style="font-family: Calibri; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-size: xx-large;">SCHOLARS &amp; THINKERS YOU NEED TO KNOW</span></h3></div><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;"><p style="font-family: Calibri; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-size: x-large; font-weight: bold;"><u><br /></u></span></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-size: x-large; font-weight: bold;"><u>BIBLICAL SCHOLARS</u></span></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 16pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;"><br /></span></p><h4 style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 16pt; margin: 0in; text-align: left;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Tim &amp; Jon at BibleProject</span></h4><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;">Tim Mackie is a Ph.D. in Semitic Languages and Biblical Studies. He is also a pastor. Jon Collins is a video creator and theological thinker. Together, they create videos, podcasts, and other biblical studies materials intended to break down the world of biblical scholarship and academia down to the layman level. Going through the podcast would be like attending bible school for free for most layman. I cannot recommend them enough.&nbsp;</p><ul><li><a href="https://bibleproject.com/">Bible Project Videos</a></li><li><a href="https://bibleproject.com/podcasts/the-bible-project-podcast/">Bible Project Podcast</a></li><li>See also, the&nbsp;<a href="https://bibleproject.com/podcasts/exploring-my-strange-bible/">Exploring My Strange Bible podcast</a>&nbsp;by Tim Mackie.</li><li>Check out his message series on&nbsp;<a href="https://bibleproject.com/podcast/series/the-amazing-jonah/">Jonah</a>.</li><li><a href="https://bibleproject.com/">BibleProject Website</a></li></ul><h4 style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 16pt; margin: 0in; text-align: left;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Michael S Heiser</span></h4><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;">Dr. Michael Heiser is most well known for his book Unseen Realm and his Naked Bible Podcast. He is a biblical scholar who focuses on removing church tradition from the interpretation of the biblical text. He is often known for his work on Angels, Demons, and Fringe material. His Naked Bible Podcast goes into depth on textual interpretation of the bible.</p><ul><li><a href="https://drmsh.com/">Heiser's Website &amp; Scholarly Articles</a></li><li><a href="https://nakedbiblepodcast.com/episodes/">The Naked Bible Podcast</a></li><ul><li>Marriage and Divorce in the&nbsp;<a href="https://nakedbiblepodcast.com/podcast/naked-bible-316-divorce-and-remarriage-in-the-old-testament/">Old Testament</a>&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;<a href="https://nakedbiblepodcast.com/podcast/naked-bible-318-divorce-and-remarriage-in-the-new-testament/">New Testament</a>.</li></ul><li>Supernatural Seminar, Celebration Church, Jackson Florida</li><ul><li><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IZKy1Xt2Ow0">Session 1</a>&nbsp;|&nbsp;<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M4pOF5oEHCE">Session 2</a>&nbsp;|&nbsp;<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sSFOZfqFcx4">Session 3</a>&nbsp;|&nbsp;<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2QBz3KsUp4U">Session 4</a></li></ul><li>FringePop321 YouTube Channel</li><ul><li><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rhIn45h9AM0">The Book of Enoch - Questions and Answers</a></li></ul></ul><div><ul><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3tEvRve">Unseen Realm</a>&nbsp;is Heiser's most popular book, it talks about what really happened in Genesis 6 and why it matters to what Jesus did. (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/36xk357">Supernatural&nbsp;</a>is the same content but at a more accessible "layman's" level. (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li><li>Angels &amp; Demons (two books) redfine everything you've been taught to believe about the beings from the other realm.&nbsp;</li><ul><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3Nfyziu" target="_blank">Angels</a>: What the Bible Really Says About God’s Heavenly Host</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3Norg86" target="_blank">Demons</a>: What the Bible Really Says About the Powers of Darkness</li></ul></ul></div><div><br /></div><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>Some favorite quotes:&nbsp;</i>Michael Heiser</p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"></p><blockquote><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>What the New Testament says about Jesus in this matrix of ideas in Christology has deep, deep Old Testament Ancient Near Eastern roots, and is not just a contrived invention.</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>&nbsp;</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>Think of the “image” of God as a verb, and you’ll get the idea. We were created to image God, to be his imagers—to represent him or be his proxy to each other and to all the earth.</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>&nbsp;</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>You are better off knowing what a passage means than you are memorizing it.</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>&nbsp;</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>Second Temple literature is really important, and therefore, we ought to pay more attention to that material for understanding the Old Testament and how the New Testament uses the Old Testament than to our own denominational traditions. Now that might sound obvious, pay attention to the ancient writers more than you do a modern writer because the ancient writers have the worldview of the cognitive frame of reference in their head. The later guys don’t. That might sound unbelievably simple and elementary and obvious but that is not intuitive for most people in church. It just isn't. They’re not told about that.They're not trained to think that way. They've never seen it modeled for them. The Bible becomes this thing that is filtered through their own personal Christian tradition and that becomes the meaning of the Bible for them. The Bible is used to reinforce something that their group or denomination or church believes. And that just isn’t Bible study. That’s not exegesis.</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>&nbsp;</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>Election and salvation are not synonyms.</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>&nbsp;</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>Yet Christians are trained to think that way about the Bible and the language of the Bible in an entirely different way from the way they use language and that's just a huge mistake. It's a fundamental flaw in the way we think about Scripture.</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>&nbsp;</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>The New Testament is essentially an inspired commentary on the Old Testament. So we need to pay attention to how the New Testament authors read the Old Testament, how they repurposed it, and their understanding of the it is not going to violate the Old Testament in its own original context. In fact, it will build on it. It will reinforce it.</i></p></blockquote><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;">&nbsp;</p><h4 style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 16pt; margin: 0in; text-align: left;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">NT Wright (Tom Wright)</span></h4><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;">NT Wright is a scholar, theologian, and Anglican Bishop. He one of the most well known New Testament scholars and has written prolifically at both the scholar-academic level and the layman level. He is often best known for his work on Paul and the New Testament for Everyone translation.</p><ul><li><a href="https://www.ntwrightonline.org/">NT Wright's Home Page</a></li><li><a href="https://www.premierchristianradio.com/Shows/Weekday/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything/Podcast">The Ask NT Wright Anything Podcast</a></li><li>Top Episodes:</li><ul><li>For best presentation on what the gospel actually means (outside of Western American Christianity):&nbsp;&nbsp;<a href="https://www.premierchristianradio.com/Shows/Weekday/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything/Podcast/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything-34-An-Evening-With-NT-Wright-live-in-London">#34 An Evening With NT Wright live in London</a></li><li>Other Favorite / Frequently Referenced Episodes:</li><li><a href="https://www.premierchristianradio.com/Shows/Weekday/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything/Podcast/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything-62-Facebook-has-questions-New-creation-souls-in-heaven-and-Torah">#62 Facebook has questions - New creation, souls in heaven and Torah</a></li><li><a href="https://www.premierchristianradio.com/Shows/Weekday/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything/Podcast/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything-58-Genesis-6-day-creation-and-the-first-humans">#58 Genesis, 6-day creation and the first humans</a></li><li><a href="https://www.premierchristianradio.com/Shows/Weekday/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything/Podcast/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything-47-Should-women-preach-and-lead-in-church-What-about-marriage">#47 Should women preach and lead in church? What about marriage?</a></li><li><a href="https://www.premierchristianradio.com/Shows/Weekday/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything/Podcast/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything-12-Genesis-evolution-Adam-and-Eve-and-The-Fall">#12 Genesis, evolution, Adam and Eve and The Fall</a></li><li><a href="https://www.premierchristianradio.com/Shows/Weekday/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything/Podcast/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything-7-Bible-infallibility-Sola-Scriptura-and-slavery">#7 Bible infallibility, Sola Scriptura and slavery</a></li><li><a href="https://www.premierchristianradio.com/Shows/Weekday/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything/Podcast/Ask-NT-Wright-Anything-1-Qs-on-Heaven-the-Kingdom-of-God-and-the-return-of-Christ">#1 Qs on Heaven, the Kingdom of God and the return of Christ</a></li></ul></ul><div>Tom's Books:</div><div><ul><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3tCyZYC">The New Testament in Its World:</a>&nbsp;An Introduction to the History, Literature, and Theology of the First Christians. NT Wright and Michael F. Bird - This book is a good entry-level summary of his lifetime's worth of work on the New Testament.</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3uxswgV" target="_blank">Surprised by Hope</a>: Rethinking Heaven, the Resurrection, and the Mission of the Church</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3qDBW9r" target="_blank">Simply Jesus</a>: A New Vision of Who He Was, What He Did, and Why He Matters</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3utvYZN" target="_blank">How God Became King</a>: The Forgotten Story of the Gospels</li><li>Warning: REALLY Heady and written to scholars not layman, but if you can follow the arguments, it's a direct rebuke of older thinking about the "Historical Jesus" scholarship.&nbsp;<a href="https://amzn.to/3izheDh" target="_blank">Jesus Victory of God V2: Christian Origins And The Question Of God</a></li></ul></div><div><br /></div><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;">Some favorite quotes: NT Wright<span style="font-size: 11pt;">&nbsp;</span></p><blockquote><i style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 14.6667px;">Traditions tell us where we have come from. Scripture itself is a better guide as to where we should be going now.</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote><i style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 14.6667px;">The great drama will end, not with "saved souls" being snatched up into heaven, away from wicked earth and the mortal bodies which have dragged them down into sin, but with the New Jerusalem coming down from heaven to earth, so that the "dwelling of God is with humans".&nbsp;</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote><i style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 14.6667px;">People who believe in the resurrection, in God making a whole new world in which everything will be set right at last, are unstoppably motivated to work for that new world in the present.</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote><i style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 14.6667px;">The whole point of the Kingdom of God is Jesus has come to bear witness to the true truth, which is nonviolent. When God wants to take charge of the world, He doesn't send in the tanks. He sends in the poor and the meek.</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>It is central to Christian living that we should celebrate the goodness of Creation, ponder its present brokenness, and insofar as we can, celebrate in advance the healing of the world, the New Creation itself. Art, music, literature, dance, theater, and many other expressions of human delight and wisdom, can all be explored in new ways.</i></p></blockquote><p>&nbsp;</p><blockquote><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>What we have in Genesis (chapter one) is a lavish description of the house that God is making for Himself to dwell in, for Himself to become known and love in His world. And at the heart of it, He puts an image, which is the living, breathing human being - male and female - who now are to reflect God into the world, and reflect the praises of creation back to God.</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>Do not despise the small but significant act. God probably does not want you to recognize the entire discipline or the entire world of your vocation overnight. Learn to be symbol-makers and story-tellers for the Kingdom of God.</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>&nbsp;</i></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><i>Jesus's first followers established a new thing - a network of worship-based, ethically rigorous, egalitarian, philanthropic, fictive kinship groups - "church" for short.</i><span style="font-size: 11pt;">&nbsp;</span></p></blockquote><h4 style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 16pt; margin: 0in; text-align: left;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Dr. John Walton</span></h4><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;">Dr. John Walton is Professor of Old Testament at&nbsp;<a href="https://www.wheaton.edu/academics/faculty/john-walton/">Wheaton College</a>, and has produced a number of books assisting the modern reader in understanding what the ancient Israelites would have instinctively known about the earliest books of the Bible.</p><ul><li>See also his&nbsp;<a href="https://www.amazon.com/John-H-Walton/e/B001IGOV8C?ref=sr_ntt_srch_lnk_3&amp;qid=1636431929&amp;sr=8-3">Amazon Profile</a>. He was one of the major contributors for the textbook&nbsp;<a href="https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B003JH836E/ref=dbs_a_def_rwt_bibl_vppi_i0">A Survey of the Old Testament</a>&nbsp;and is often known for his book&nbsp;<a href="https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B003VM8QK0/ref=dbs_a_def_rwt_bibl_vppi_i1">The Lost World of Genesis One</a>.</li><li>See also, his guest appearances on the Phil Vischer/Holy Post Podcast. (Phil Vischer Podcast (eventually rebranded as Holy Post)):</li><li><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KciqJt-9MXM">The Phil Vischer Podcast, Episode 80: Science vs. the Bible with John Walton</a></li><li><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cLM0KU9xBuQ">The Phil Vischer Podcast, Episode 146: The Lost World of Adam and Eve!</a></li><li><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cLM0KU9xBuQ">The Phil Vischer Podcast, Episode 160: Back to the Lost World of Adam and Eve!</a></li><li>Holy Post:</li><li><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7WY6lEV36yI">Episode 208: Talking About Hell! With John Walton</a></li><li><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=enO1vq6Q5Cg">The Phil Vischer Podcast, Episode 123: The Book of Job with Guest John Walton</a></li></ul><h4 style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 16pt; margin: 0in; text-align: left;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Dr.&nbsp;</span><b>David A. deSilva, Ph.D.</b></h4><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;">Dr. deSilva is a Professor of&nbsp;New Testament, Greek, and Second Temple studies at&nbsp;<a href="https://seminary.ashland.edu/faculty-and-staff/david-desilva/" target="_blank">Ashland Theological Seminary</a>. Professor deSilva is widely published both at the academic and popular level, including a few novels based in the biblical era. He was interviewed several times by Dr. Heiser on the Naked Bible Podcast, as well as others.&nbsp;</p><ul><li>See also his&nbsp;<a href="https://www.amazon.com/David-Arthur-DeSilva/e/B001HCW0R6%3Fref=dbs_a_mng_rwt_scns_share">Amazon Profile</a>. He was&nbsp;</li><li>Seedbed:&nbsp;<a href="https://youtu.be/lFBfDQLTgk8" target="_blank">Why Christians Should Read the Apocrypha</a>&nbsp;(David deSilva)</li><li><a href="https://www.youtube.com/user/ashlandseminary" target="_blank">Ashland Theological Seminary</a>:&nbsp;<a href="https://youtu.be/rsS5dzyUguU" target="_blank">Understanding Biblical Prophecy and the End Times-Speaker View</a>, April 26, 2021</li><li>Book:&nbsp;<a href="https://amzn.to/3J8TMsI" target="_blank">A Week In the Life of Ephesus (A Week in the Life Series)</a>&nbsp;</li></ul><h4 style="font-family: Calibri; margin: 0in; text-align: left;"><b><span style="font-size: large;">Dr. Craig Keener</span></b></h4>From his website: "Dr. Keener did his Ph.D. work in New Testament and Christian Origins at Duke University and is known for his work as a New Testament scholar on Bible background (commentaries on the New Testament in its early Jewish and Greco-Roman settings). Well over a million of his thirty-plus books are in circulation and have won thirteen national and international awards." - (some Amazon affiliate links)<br /><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Check out his free resources <a href="https://craigkeener.com/free-resources/">HERE</a>.</li><li>Especially - <a href="https://craigkeener.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/11/Bible-In-Its-Context-revised-8-14-14-for-Snowfall.pdf">The Bible in Its Context</a>: How to Improve Your Study of the Scriptures</li><li><a href="https://craigkeener.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/EJ_2001_02_Spring_Keener-1.pdf">Women in Ministry</a></li></ul><br /><p style="margin: 0in;"></p><h4 style="margin: 0in; text-align: left;"><span style="font-family: Calibri;"><span style="font-size: 21.3333px;"><b>Paula Fredriksen</b></span></span></h4><div>From Faculty Page: "Paula Fredriksen, the Aurelio Professor of Scripture emerita at Boston University, since 2009 has been Distinguished Visiting Professor of Comparative Religion at the Hebrew University, Jerusalem."</div><div><br /></div><div>Possibly best known for her book:</div></div><blockquote style="border: none; margin: 0px 0px 0px 40px; padding: 0px; text-align: left;"><div style="margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;"><a href="https://www.amazon.com/Paul-Pagans-Apostle-Paula-Fredriksen/dp/0300225881">Paul: The Pagans’ Apostle</a></div><div style="margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;">By Paula Fredriksen</div><div style="margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;">Yale University Press</div><div style="margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;">August 22, 2017</div><div style="margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;">Buy it now from <a href="https://www.amazon.com/Paul-Pagans-Apostle-Paula-Fredriksen/dp/0300225881">Amazon!</a></div></blockquote><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;"><div><br /></div><div>Fredriksen has many of her Journal Articles available on her bio faculty page, here are a few highlights:</div><br /><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Fredriksen, Paula. “<a href="https://www.bu.edu/religion/files/2010/03/Paul-the-10-Commandments-and-Pagan-%E2%80%98Justification-by-Faith..pdf" target="_blank">Paul, the 10 Commandments, and Pagan ‘Justification by Faith</a>.” Journal of Biblical Literature 133, no. 4 (2014): 801–8. https://www.bu.edu/religion/files/2010/03/Paul-the-10-Commandments-and-Pagan-%E2%80%98Justification-by-Faith..pdf.</li></ul><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Fredriksen, Paula. “<a href="https://www.bu.edu/religion/files/2019/02/JJMJS.pdf" target="_blank">Putting Paul in His (Historical) Place</a>: A Response to James Crossley, Margaret Mitchell, and Matthew Novenson.” Journal of the Jesus Movement in It’s Jewish Setting, no. 5 (2018): 89–110.</li></ul><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Fredriksen, Paula. “<a href="https://www.bu.edu/religion/files/2015/12/PF-Law-free-Paul-JBL-134-2015-637-501.pdf" target="_blank">Why Should a ‘Law-Free’ Mission Mean a ‘Law-Free’ Apostle?</a>” Journal of Biblical Literature 134, no. 3 (2015): 637–50. https://www.bu.edu/religion/files/2015/12/PF-Law-free-Paul-JBL-134-2015-637-501.pdf.</li></ul><div><br /></div><br /><br /><br /><p style="font-family: Calibri; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-size: x-large; font-weight: bold;"><u>MENTAL HEALTH</u></span></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 16pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;"><br /></span></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 16pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Bob Hamp | Think Differently</span></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;">Bob Hamp, LMFT is a licensed mental health provider and former pastor. After creating the Freedom Ministries department for Gateway Church, he went back to private practice and teaching the core principles around "Thinking Differently".</p><ul><li><a href="https://tdacad.com/">Think Differently Academy</a>; virtual community, books, and courses designed to transform your life by changing not "what" you think but "how" you think.</li><li>Bob Hamp's Foundations of Freedom videos changed the course of my life. They can be found on his website by signing up for any of the plans (<a href="https://tdacad.com/pricing-plan/">here</a>) or the older/original videos can be found on&nbsp;<a href="https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLnIIzkbSbhmULFuueFoyftz6iJlQog3ww">YouTube (here)</a>.</li><li>If you happen to be in DFW,&nbsp;<a href="https://tdcounseling.com/staff/">Think Differently Counseling</a>, Southlake Texas</li></ul><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 16pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Dr. Henry Cloud</span></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;">Dr Henry Cloud may be the world's most well known Christian Psychologist. Best known for his work on Boundaries, which is a must-read for pretty much every human being alive today.</p><ul><li>Books:</li><li><a href="https://www.amazon.com/Boundaries-Updated-Expanded-When-Control/dp/0310351804/ref=pd_bxgy_img_1/138-7573816-9951565?pd_rd_w=6YheD&amp;pf_rd_p=fd3ebcd0-c1a2-44cf-aba2-bbf4810b3732&amp;pf_rd_r=EB2V7V55PY48MMJ3B5Z5&amp;pd_rd_r=af59c66a-3973-401d-bbe1-d43ef3b031a1&amp;pd_rd_wg=HuFol&amp;pd_rd_i=0310351804&amp;psc=1">Boundaries&nbsp;</a>Updated and Expanded Edition: When to Say Yes, How to Say No To Take Control of Your Life. Drs. Henry Cloud and John Townsend</li><li><a href="https://www.amazon.com/How-to-Get-Date-Worth-Keeping-audiobook/dp/B004JC810G/ref=sr_1_1?dchild=1&amp;keywords=how+to+get+a+date+worth+keeping&amp;qid=1624084025&amp;sr=8-1">How to Get a Date Worth Keeping</a>, Dr Henry Cloud</li><li><a href="https://www.drcloud.com/">His Personal Website</a></li><li>The&nbsp;<a href="https://www.boundaries.me/">Boundaries.Me</a>&nbsp;website is a subscription site where you can join a community and take several online classes related mental health and counseling.</li></ul><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></p><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="font-size: large;">OTHER THINKERS, BOOKS, &amp; BLOGS</span></h4><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;"><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;"><div><b>BIBLICAL SCHOLARSHIP BOOKS</b></div><ul><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3urVdeT">The Pentateuch as Narrative</a>: A Biblical-Theological Commentary (1995) ISBN 978-0310574217, by&nbsp;John Sailhamer (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3iA9tN9">Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus</a>: How the Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your Faith, by Ann Spangler (Author), Lois Tverberg (Author) (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3ulWNiw">Grasping God’s Word</a>: A Hand-On Approach to Reading, Interpreting, and Applying the Bible. 4th Edition. Grand Rapids: Zondervan, 2012. ISBN 978-0-310-10917-4, by Daniel Hayes and J. Scott Duvall (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3LjYpjL">Reading and Understanding the Bible.&nbsp;</a>New York: Oxford University Press, 2015. ISBN 978-0199340576, by Ben Witherington III (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3iHTTPv">How to Read the Bible for All It’s Worth.</a>&nbsp;Grand Rapids: Zondervan, 2003., by Gordon Fee and Douglas Stuart (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3uwsGF2">How to Read the Bible Book by Book</a>: A Guided Tour, by Gordon Fee and Douglas Stuart (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3JWlatT">A Basic Guide to Interpreting the Bible: Playing by the Rules</a>. Grand Rapids: Baker, 1994, by Robert Stein (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3wHs4PF">Elements of a Christian Worldview</a>&nbsp;by Michael Palmer (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3tEtYid">Saving the Bible from Ourselves</a>: Learning to Read and Live the Bible Well by Glenn R. Paauw (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/381XtSK" target="_blank">Israel Matters</a>: Why Christians Must Think Differently about the People and the Land, by Gerald R. McDermott&nbsp; (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li></ul><div><br /></div><div><br /></div></div><p style="font-family: Calibri; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-size: 21.3333px;"><b>FICTION BOOKS</b></span></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-size: 11pt;"><br /></span></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-size: 11pt;">A narrative/story often gets to the heart in ways non-fiction just can't. This is one reason Jesus told parables in his teacing (though his reasons were many and more complicated). Here are some fiction books that helped me see the heart of God for his humans:</span></p><p style="font-family: Calibri; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-size: 11pt;"><br /></span></p><b>BIBLICAL FICTION</b><p style="font-family: Calibri; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-size: 11pt;"><b><br /></b></span></p><span style="font-weight: bold;">Single Titles:</span><ul><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3uwtgTe">TheShack</a>, by William P. Young (<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)</li></ul><span style="font-weight: bold;">Author Ted Dekker&nbsp;</span>(<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)<ul><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3LgSOe0">TheCircle Series</a>&nbsp;(White, Green, Red, Black), by Ted Dekker</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3NfY4QV">Thr3e</a>, by Ted Dekker</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3IzSKUJ">Hacker:The Outlaw Chronicles</a>, by Ted Dekker</li></ul><span style="font-weight: bold;">Author Bill Myers&nbsp;</span>(<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)<ul><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3JGLhop">Eli</a>, by Bill Myers</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3NhQQfj">Bloodof Heaven (Fire of Heaven Trilogy Book 1)</a>, Bill Myers</li></ul><b>Author Lauren Stinton&nbsp;</b>(<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)<b><br /></b><ul><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3JBFSPk">TheHouse of Elah</a>&nbsp;(3 book series), by Lauren Stinton</li><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3tEDwcR">TheHamal Books</a>, by Lauren Stinton</li></ul><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;"><br /></div><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;"><b>SECULAR FICTION</b></div>And... some fiction books that are not explicitly biblical but helped me enjoy that part of our reality that we all feel but can't touch, that part just beyond the veil.<br /></div><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;"><br /></div><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.4291in;"><b>Author K.M Wieland&nbsp;</b>(<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)<b><br /></b><ul><li><a href="https://amzn.to/3iDDYli">Dreamlander</a>, by K.M. Wieland</li></ul><b>Author Brandon Sanderson&nbsp;</b>(<i>Amazon affiliate link</i>)<ul><li><a href="https://amzn.to/37UYls5">Mistborn (series)</a>, by Brandon Sanderson</li></ul><p style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><br style="font-size: 14.6667px;" /></p></div><h3 style="font-family: Calibri; margin: 0in;"></h3></div>
